claim: Real-world AI agents act as coordination layers orchestrating actions across multiple systems while maintaining context, enforcing rules, and determining when to transition control to humans
actionable: Design agents as orchestration layers with explicit handoff points rather than autonomous decision-makers in production workflows
source_title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows

claim: Agents handling complex tasks like employee onboarding must sequence actions across systems while managing state, timing, rules, and exceptions without replacing human oversight
actionable: Build onboarding agents with context-based signals (role, location, start date) to sequence multi-system provisioning while flagging deviations
source_title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows

claim: Policy-governed agents in IT support must evaluate applicable policies, automatically execute permitted actions, and escalate ambiguous or high-risk cases
actionable: Design agent workflows with explicit policy evaluation gates that automatically execute low-risk actions while escalating high-impact requests
source_title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows

claim: Successful production agents are narrowly scoped, orchestrate across systems, apply rules, relay context signals, keep humans in the loop, and are designed for integration not isolation
actionable: When scoping production agent work, constrain boundaries, integrate with existing systems, define clear escalation rules, and design for human-in-the-loop decision points
source_title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows

claim: Real complexity in agent workflows lies in handling missing data, mismatched data, and non-standard conditions; agents add value by consistently handling predictable flows while surfacing exceptions for human review
actionable: Design agents to handle the happy path efficiently while providing detailed context for exception cases that humans will review
source_title: Building AI Agents for Real-World Problems & Workflows
